narratives.population: Population grew by 12,804 residents since 2010 while average age remained above 45 years — deaths exceeded births in 15 of the last 15 years.
narratives.emergency: Emergency deployments rose 5% since 2010 while population grew 6% — incident volumes remain persistently high for a slowly growing city.
narratives.healthcare: Altstadt has 33× more physicians per capita than Prester — access depends on where you live.
narratives.insights: Natural population decline continues despite net migration of +3,550 in 2024.
narratives.risk: Neue Neustadt ranks highest for healthcare vulnerability — combining aging residents, physician scarcity, and population scale.

healthcare.excludedDistrictsNote: 2 districts with fewer than 1,000 residents or no physician registrations in KISS-MD are excluded from access rankings.

keyFindings.title: Deaths exceeded births every year
keyFindings.body: In all 15 years since 2010, Magdeburg recorded more deaths than births — a persistent natural population deficit.

keyFindings.title: Emergency demand remains high despite stable population
keyFindings.body: Emergency incidents stayed near 53,310 per year in 2024 despite only 6% population growth since 2010.

keyFindings.title: Healthcare access differs sharply by district
keyFindings.body: Physician density spans 0.41 to 13.70 per 1,000 residents across districts — a gap of 13.29.

keyFindings.title: Migration offsets population decline
keyFindings.body: Net migration of +3,550 in 2024 keeps total population growing, but does not reverse the underlying aging trend.

riskDistricts.rationale: high elderly share, large resident base
